focus: adversarial re-verification of P1-001 (lease-use/generation atomicity) — three interleaving scenarios adjudicated

scenarios.name: refresh_late_arrival_heartbeat
scenarios.damage: successor self-terminates via heartbeat fence L595-601; no data corruption

scenarios.name: clear_cross_process
scenarios.damage: successor L2 deleted; successor self-terminates via heartbeat fence L595-601; no data corruption

findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: If implementer extends §13 to explicitly acknowledge refresh/clear windows as same-class-accepted-risk AND either moves reread to pre-write position OR adopts flock on the lease file, P1-001 downgrades to P2
findingDetails.refinedFraming: Same-class TOCTOU as §13 item 1 (already acknowledged for ACQUIRE path); documentation-completeness finding more than code-correctness finding

claim: refreshOwnerLeaseFile (L562-579) and clearOwnerLeaseFile/clearOwnerLeaseFileIfOwner (L611-638) validate a generation (leaseId, ownerPid) before mutation but do not atomically bind the later replace/unlink to that generation. A stale writer can mutate a successor's lease in a sub-microsecond OS-level window between the second-read fence and the write/unlink syscall.
counterevidenceSought: Is there a pre-write reread? NO
Is the L575-576 reread sufficient? NO
Is the L616 reread sufficient? NO
Could the heartbeat fence at L595-601 make this moot? PARTIALLY
Is the same-process scenario a real risk? NO
alternativeExplanation: Most likely: implementer understood refresh/clear TOCTOU windows are SAME class as ACQUIRE window already documented in §13 item 1 and consciously extended accepted-risk framing. §13 acknowledgement should be tightened to explicitly include all three lease-mutation paths. Less likely: implementer focused on ACQUIRE because that's where original spec language pointed, and didn't independently reason about refresh/clear. Either way, underlying safety story is intact.
downgradeTrigger: If implementer (a) extends §13 to explicitly acknowledge refresh/clear windows as accepted-risk same-class, AND (b) either moves reread to pre-write position OR adopts flock on the lease file, P1-001 downgrades to P2

damageDescription: B's fresh L2 lease is overwritten by A's stale heartbeat at L570; A's reread at L575 sees A's own write and returns true. B's next heartbeat fails (ownerPid mismatch), B self-terminates via L595-601. No data corruption; SQLite sidecar is the real integrity backstop.
sameAsAcceptdRisk: YES — same TOCTOU class as implementer's §13 item 1 (ACQUIRE path window between classification read and unlink syscall)
testCoverage: NOT COVERED — launcher-spec-memory-lifecycle.vitest.ts:114-161 only tests ACQUIRE reclaim, NOT refresh

damageDescription: A's L616 second-read returns L1 (A's), then B reclaims in the gap (unlinks L1, writes L2). A's L617 unlinkSync removes B's L2. B's next heartbeat fails (no lease file), B self-terminates via L595-601. No data corruption.
sameAsAcceptdRisk: YES — same TOCTOU class as §13 item 1
testCoverage: NOT COVERED — no test exercises clear TOCTOU path

reason: Node single-threaded event loop rules out structurally. refreshOwnerLeaseFile (L562-579) is fully synchronous with no await/setTimeout. acquireOwnerLeaseFile (L512-560) is also fully synchronous. They are mutually exclusive within a single tick.

notes: iter 8 does not re-test spec_code; iter 6 results stand. REQ-010's complete-fencing claim remains bounded by active P1-001.
iter 8 does not re-test checklist; iter 6 results stand. CHK-074 (REQ-010 complete-fencing) scope remains bounded by P1-001.

p1DowngradeCandidatesRejected: P2 (until §13 is extended or fences are tightened)
closed (two scenarios ARE exploitable in sub-microsecond windows)

Also check no BANNED file mutations occurred anywhere in this review session.
